verses.latin_vulgate: Et quidam descendentes de Judæa docebant fratres: Quia nisi circumcidamini secundum morem Moysi, non potestis salvari.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καί τινες κατελθόντες ἀπὸ τῆς ἰουδαίας ἐδίδασκον τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς ὅτι ἐὰν μὴ περιτέμνησθε τῷ ἔθει μωϋσέως οὐ δύνασθε σωθῆναι
verses.king_james: And certain men which came down from Judaea taught the brethren, *and said*, Except ye be circumcised after the manner of Moses, ye cannot be saved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facta ergo seditione non minima Paulo et Barnabæ adversus illos, statuerunt ut ascenderent Paulus et Barnabas, et quidam alii ex aliis ad Apostolos et presbyteros in Jerusalem super hac quæstione.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γενομένης οὖν στάσεως καὶ συζητήσεως οὐκ ὀλίγης τῷ παύλῳ καὶ τῷ βαρναβᾷ πρὸς αὐτοὺς ἔταξαν ἀναβαίνειν παῦλον καὶ βαρναβᾶν καί τινας ἄλλους ἐξ αὐτῶν πρὸς τοὺς ἀποστόλους καὶ πρεσβυτέρους εἰς ἰερουσαλὴμ περὶ τοῦ ζητήματος τούτου
verses.king_james: When therefore Paul and Barnabas had no small dissension and disputation with them, they determined that Paul and Barnabas, and certain other of them, should go up to Jerusalem unto the apostles and elders about this question.

verses.latin_vulgate: Illi ergo deducti ab ecclesia pertransibant Phœnicen et Samariam, narrantes conversionem gentium: et faciebant gaudium magnum omnibus fratribus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ μὲν οὖν προπεμφθέντες ὑπὸ τῆς ἐκκλησίας διήρχοντο τήν φοινίκην καὶ σαμάρειαν ἐκδιηγούμενοι τὴν ἐπιστροφὴν τῶν ἐθνῶν καὶ ἐποίουν χαρὰν μεγάλην πᾶσιν τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς
verses.king_james: And being brought on their way by the church, they passed through Phenice and Samaria, declaring the conversion of the Gentiles: and they caused great joy unto all the brethren.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem venissent Jerosolymam, suscepti sunt ab ecclesia, et ab Apostolis et senioribus, annuntiantes quanta Deus fecisset cum illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παραγενόμενοι δὲ εἰς ἰερουσαλὴμ ἀπεδέχθησαν ὑπὸ τῆς ἐκκλησίας καὶ τῶν ἀποστόλων καὶ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων ἀνήγγειλάν τε ὅσα ὁ θεὸς ἐποίησεν μετ᾽ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And when they were come to Jerusalem, they were received of the church, and *of* he apostles and elders, and they declared all things that God had done with them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surrexerunt autem quidam de hæresi pharisæorum, qui crediderunt, dicentes quia oportet circumcidi eos, præcipere quoque servare legem Moysi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξανέστησαν δέ τινες τῶν ἀπὸ τῆς αἱρέσεως τῶν φαρισαίων πεπιστευκότες λέγοντες ὅτι δεῖ περιτέμνειν αὐτοὺς παραγγέλλειν τε τηρεῖν τὸν νόμον μωϋσέως
verses.king_james: But there rose up certain of the sect of the Pharisees which believed, saying, That it was needful to circumcise them, and to command *them* to keep the law of Moses.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conveneruntque Apostoli et seniores videre de verbo hoc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνήχθησάν δὲ οἱ ἀπόστολοι καὶ οἱ πρεσβύτεροι ἰδεῖν περὶ τοῦ λόγου τούτου
verses.king_james: And the apostles and elders came together for to consider of this matter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem magna conquisitio fieret, surgens Petrus dixit ad eos: Viri fratres, vos scitis quoniam ab antiquis diebus Deus in nobis elegit, per os meum audire gentes verbum Evangelii et credere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλῆς δὲ συζητήσεως γενομένης ἀναστὰς πέτρος εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς ἄνδρες ἀδελφοί ὑμεῖς ἐπίστασθε ὅτι ἀφ᾽ ἡμερῶν ἀρχαίων ὁ θεὸς ἐν ἡμῖν ἐξελέξατο διὰ τοῦ στόματός μου ἀκοῦσαι τὰ ἔθνη τὸν λόγον τοῦ εὐαγγελίου καὶ πιστεῦσαι
verses.king_james: And when there had been much disputing, Peter rose up, and said unto them, Men *and* brethren, ye know how that a good while ago God made choice among us, that the Gentiles by my mouth should hear the word of the gospel, and believe.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui novit corda Deus, testimonium perhibuit, dans illis Spiritum Sanctum, sicut et nobis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ καρδιογνώστης θεὸς ἐμαρτύρησεν αὐτοῖς δοὺς αὐτοῖς, τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον καθὼς καὶ ἡμῖν
verses.king_james: And God, which knoweth the hearts, bare them witness, giving them the Holy Ghost, even as *he did* unto us;

verses.latin_vulgate: et nihil discrevit inter nos et illos, fide purificans corda eorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐδέν διέκρινεν μεταξὺ ἡμῶν τε καὶ αὐτῶν τῇ πίστει καθαρίσας τὰς καρδίας αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And put no difference between us and them, purifying their hearts by faith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc ergo quid tentatis Deum, imponere jugum super cervices discipulorum quod neque patres nostri, neque nos portare potuimus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νῦν οὖν τί πειράζετε τὸν θεόν ἐπιθεῖναι ζυγὸν ἐπὶ τὸν τράχηλον τῶν μαθητῶν ὃν οὔτε οἱ πατέρες ἡμῶν οὔτε ἡμεῖς ἰσχύσαμεν βαστάσαι
verses.king_james: Now therefore why tempt ye God, to put a yoke upon the neck of the disciples, which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear?

verses.latin_vulgate: sed per gratiam Domini Jesu Christi credimus salvari, quemadmodum et illi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ διὰ τῆς χάριτος κυρίου ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ πιστεύομεν σωθῆναι καθ᾽ ὃν τρόπον κἀκεῖνοι
verses.king_james: But we believe that through the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ we shall be saved, even as they.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tacuit autem omnis multitudo: et audiebant Barnabam et Paulum narrantes quanta Deus fecisset signa et prodigia per eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐσίγησεν δὲ πᾶν τὸ πλῆθος καὶ ἤκουον βαρναβᾶ καὶ παύλου ἐξηγουμένων ὅσα ἐποίησεν ὁ θεὸς σημεῖα καὶ τέρατα ἐν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν δι᾽ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Then all the multitude kept silence, and gave audience to Barnabas and Paul, declaring what miracles and wonders God had wrought among the Gentiles by them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et postquam tacuerunt, respondit Jacobus, dicens: Viri fratres, audite me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ τὸ σιγῆσαι αὐτοὺς ἀπεκρίθη ἰάκωβος λέγων, ἄνδρες ἀδελφοί ἀκούσατέ μου
verses.king_james: And after they had held their peace, James answered, saying, Men *and* brethren, hearken unto me:

verses.latin_vulgate: Simon narravit quemadmodum primum Deus visitavit sumere ex gentibus populum nomini suo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συμεὼν ἐξηγήσατο καθὼς πρῶτον ὁ θεὸς ἐπεσκέψατο λαβεῖν ἐξ ἐθνῶν λαὸν ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματι αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Simeon hath declared how God at the first did visit the Gentiles, to take out of them a people for his name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et huic concordant verba prophetarum: sicut scriptum est:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τούτῳ συμφωνοῦσιν οἱ λόγοι τῶν προφητῶν καθὼς γέγραπται
verses.king_james: And to this agree the words of the prophets; as it is written,

verses.latin_vulgate: [Post hæc revertar, et reædificabo tabernaculum David quod decidit: et diruta ejus reædificabo, et erigam illud:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ ταῦτα ἀναστρέψω καὶ ἀνοικοδομήσω τὴν σκηνὴν δαβὶδ τὴν πεπτωκυῖαν καὶ τὰ κατεσκαμμένα αὐτῆς ἀνοικοδομήσω καὶ ἀνορθώσω αὐτήν
verses.king_james: After this I will return, and will build again the tabernacle of David, which is fallen down; and I will build again the ruins thereof, and I will set it up:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut requirant ceteri hominum Dominum, et omnes gentes super quas invocatum est nomen meum, dicit Dominus faciens hæc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅπως ἂν ἐκζητήσωσιν οἱ κατάλοιποι τῶν ἀνθρώπων τὸν κύριον καὶ πάντα τὰ ἔθνη ἐφ᾽ οὓς ἐπικέκληται τὸ ὄνομά μου ἐπ᾽ αὐτούς λέγει κύριος ὁ ποιῶν ταῦτα πάντα
verses.king_james: That the residue of men might seek after the Lord, and all the Gentiles, upon whom my name is called, saith the Lord, who doeth all these things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Notum a sæculo est Domino opus suum.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γνωστὰ ἀπ᾽ αἰῶνος ἐστίν τῷ θεῷ πάντα τὰ ἔργα αὑτοῦ
verses.king_james: Known unto God are all his works from the beginning of the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod ego judico non inquietari eos qui ex gentibus convertuntur ad Deum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ ἐγὼ κρίνω μὴ παρενοχλεῖν τοῖς ἀπὸ τῶν ἐθνῶν ἐπιστρέφουσιν ἐπὶ τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: Wherefore my sentence is, that we trouble not them, which from among the Gentiles are turned to God:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed scribere ad eos ut abstineant se a contaminationibus simulacrorum, et fornicatione, et suffocatis, et sanguine.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ ἐπιστεῖλαι αὐτοῖς τοῦ ἀπέχεσθαι ἀπό τῶν ἀλισγημάτων τῶν εἰδώλων καὶ τῆς πορνείας καὶ τοῦ πνικτοῦ καὶ τοῦ αἵματος
verses.king_james: But that we write unto them, that they abstain from pollutions of idols, and *from* fornication, and *from* things strangled, and *from* blood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Moyses enim a temporibus antiquis habet in singulis civitatibus qui eum prædicent in synagogis, ubi per omne sabbatum legitur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μωσῆς γὰρ ἐκ γενεῶν ἀρχαίων κατὰ πόλιν τοὺς κηρύσσοντας αὐτὸν ἔχει ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς κατὰ πᾶν σάββατον ἀναγινωσκόμενος
verses.king_james: For Moses of old time hath in every city them that preach him, being read in the synagogues every sabbath day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc placuit Apostolis et senioribus cum omni ecclesia eligere viros ex eis, et mittere Antiochiam cum Paulo et Barnaba: Judam, qui cognominabatur Barsabas, et Silam, viros primos in fratribus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἔδοξεν τοῖς ἀποστόλοις καὶ τοῖς πρεσβυτέροις σὺν ὅλῃ τῇ ἐκκλησίᾳ ἐκλεξαμένους ἄνδρας ἐξ αὐτῶν πέμψαι εἰς ἀντιόχειαν σὺν τῷ παύλῳ καὶ βαρναβᾷ ἰούδαν τὸν ἐπικαλούμενον βαρσαβᾶν καὶ σιλᾶν ἄνδρας ἡγουμένους ἐν τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς
verses.king_james: Then pleased it the apostles and elders, with the whole church, to send chosen men of their own company to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas; *namely*, Judas surnamed Barsabas, and Silas, chief men among the brethren:

verses.latin_vulgate: scribentes per manus eorum: Apostoli et seniores fratres, his qui sunt Antiochiæ, et Syriæ, et Ciliciæ, fratribus ex gentibus, salutem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γράψαντες διὰ χειρὸς αὐτῶν τάδε, οἱ ἀπόστολοι καὶ οἱ πρεσβύτεροι καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ τοῖς κατὰ τὴν ἀντιόχειαν καὶ συρίαν καὶ κιλικίαν ἀδελφοῖς τοῖς ἐξ ἐθνῶν χαίρειν
verses.king_james: And they wrote *letters* by them after this manner; The apostles and elders and brethren *send* greeting unto the brethren which are of the Gentiles in Antioch and Syria and Cilicia:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam audivimus quia quidam ex nobis exeuntes, turbaverunt vos verbis, evertentes animas vestras, quibus non mandavimus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπειδὴ ἠκούσαμεν ὅτι τινὲς ἐξ ἡμῶν ἐξελθόντες ἐτάραξαν ὑμᾶς λόγοις ἀνασκευάζοντες τὰς ψυχὰς ὑμῶν λέγοντες περιτέμνεσθαι καὶ τηρεῖν τον νόμον, οἷς οὐ διεστειλάμεθα
verses.king_james: Forasmuch as we have heard, that certain which went out from us have troubled you with words, subverting your souls, saying, *Ye must* be circumcised, and keep the law: to whom we gave no *such* commandment:

verses.latin_vulgate: placuit nobis collectis in unum eligere viros, et mittere ad vos cum carissimis nostris Barnaba et Paulo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔδοξεν ἡμῖν γενομένοις ὁμοθυμαδὸν ἐκλεξαμένους ἄνδρας πέμψαι πρὸς ὑμᾶς σὺν τοῖς ἀγαπητοῖς ἡμῶν βαρναβᾷ καὶ παύλῳ
verses.king_james: It seemed good unto us, being assembled with one accord, to send chosen men unto you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,

verses.latin_vulgate: hominibus qui tradiderunt animas suas pro nomine Domini nostri Jesu Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνθρώποις παραδεδωκόσιν τὰς ψυχὰς αὐτῶν ὑπὲρ τοῦ ὀνόματος τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Men that have hazarded their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misimus ergo Judam et Silam, qui et ipsi vobis verbis referent eadem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεστάλκαμεν οὖν ἰούδαν καὶ σιλᾶν καὶ αὐτοὺς διὰ λόγου ἀπαγγέλλοντας τὰ αὐτά
verses.king_james: We have sent therefore Judas and Silas, who shall also tell *you* the same things by mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Visum est enim Spiritui Sancto et nobis nihil ultra imponere vobis oneris quam hæc necessaria:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔδοξεν γὰρ τῷ ἁγίῳ πνεύματι καὶ ἡμῖν μηδὲν πλέον ἐπιτίθεσθαι ὑμῖν βάρος πλὴν τῶν ἐπάναγκες τούτων
verses.king_james: For it seemed good to the Holy Ghost, and to us, to lay upon you no greater burden than these necessary things;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut abstineatis vos ab immolatis simulacrorum, et sanguine, et suffocato, et fornicatione: a quibus custodientes vos, bene agetis. Valete.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπέχεσθαι εἰδωλοθύτων καὶ αἵματος καὶ πνικτοῦ καὶ πορνείας ἐξ ὧν διατηροῦντες ἑαυτοὺς εὖ πράξετε ἔῤῥωσθε
verses.king_james: That ye abstain from meats offered to idols, and from blood, and from things strangled, and from fornication: from which if ye keep yourselves, ye shall do well. Fare ye well.

verses.latin_vulgate: Illi ergo dimissi, descenderunt Antiochiam: et congregata multitudine tradiderunt epistolam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ μὲν οὖν ἀπολυθέντες ἦλθον εἰς ἀντιόχειαν καὶ συναγαγόντες τὸ πλῆθος ἐπέδωκαν τὴν ἐπιστολήν
verses.king_james: So when they were dismissed, they came to Antioch: and when they had gathered the multitude together, they delivered the epistle:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam cum legissent, gavisi sunt super consolatione.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀναγνόντες δὲ ἐχάρησαν ἐπὶ τῇ παρακλήσει
verses.king_james: *Which* when they had read, they rejoiced for the consolation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judas autem et Silas, et ipsi cum essent prophetæ, verbo plurimo consolati sunt fratres, et confirmaverunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰούδας τε καὶ σιλᾶς καὶ αὐτοὶ προφῆται ὄντες διὰ λόγου πολλοῦ παρεκάλεσαν τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς καὶ ἐπεστήριξαν
verses.king_james: And Judas and Silas, being prophets also themselves, exhorted the brethren with many words, and confirmed *them*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facto autem ibi aliquanto tempore, dimissi sunt cum pace a fratribus ad eos qui miserant illos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ποιήσαντες δὲ χρόνον ἀπελύθησαν μετ᾽ εἰρήνης ἀπὸ τῶν ἀδελφῶν πρὸς τοὺς ἀποστόλους
verses.king_james: And after they had tarried *there* a space, they were let go in peace from the brethren unto the apostles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Visum est autem Silæ ibi remanere: Judas autem solus abiit Jerusalem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔδοξεν δὲ τῷ σίλᾳ ἐπιμεῖναι αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Notwithstanding it pleased Silas to abide there still.

verses.latin_vulgate: Paulus autem et Barnabas demorabantur Antiochiæ, docentes et evangelizantes cum aliis pluribus verbum Domini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παῦλος δὲ καὶ βαρναβᾶς διέτριβον ἐν ἀντιοχείᾳ διδάσκοντες καὶ εὐαγγελιζόμενοι μετὰ καὶ ἑτέρων πολλῶν τὸν λόγον τοῦ κυρίου
verses.king_james: Paul also and Barnabas continued in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord, with many others also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post aliquot autem dies, dixit ad Barnabam Paulus: Revertentes visitemus fratres per universas civitates in quibus prædicavimus verbum Domini, quomodo se habeant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δέ τινας ἡμέρας εἶπεν παῦλος πρὸς βαρναβᾶν ἐπιστρέψαντες δὴ ἐπισκεψώμεθα τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς ἡμῶν κατὰ πᾶσαν πόλιν ἐν αἷς κατηγγείλαμεν τὸν λόγον τοῦ κυρίου πῶς ἔχουσιν
verses.king_james: And some days after Paul said unto Barnabas, Let us go again and visit our brethren in every city where we have preached the word of the Lord, *and see* how they do.

verses.latin_vulgate: Barnabas autem volebat secum assumere et Joannem, qui cognominabatur Marcus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βαρναβᾶς δὲ ἐβούλεύσατο συμπαραλαβεῖν τὸν ἰωάννην τὸν καλούμενον μᾶρκον
verses.king_james: And Barnabas determined to take with them John, whose surname was Mark.

verses.latin_vulgate: Paulus autem rogabat eum (ut qui discessisset ab eis de Pamphylia, et non isset cum eis in opus) non debere recipi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παῦλος δὲ ἠξίου τὸν ἀποστάντα ἀπ᾽ αὐτῶν ἀπὸ παμφυλίας καὶ μὴ συνελθόντα αὐτοῖς εἰς τὸ ἔργον μὴ συμπαραλαβεῖν τοῦτον
verses.king_james: But Paul thought not good to take him with them, who departed from them from Pamphylia, and went not with them to the work.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facta est autem dissensio, ita ut discederent ab invicem, et Barnabas quidem, assumpto Marco, navigaret Cyprum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο οὖν παροξυσμὸς ὥστε ἀποχωρισθῆναι αὐτοὺς ἀπ᾽ ἀλλήλων τόν τε βαρναβᾶν παραλαβόντα τὸν μᾶρκον ἐκπλεῦσαι εἰς κύπρον
verses.king_james: And the contention was so sharp between them, that they departed asunder one from the other: and so Barnabas took Mark, and sailed unto Cyprus;

verses.latin_vulgate: Paulus vero, electo Sila, profectus est, traditus gratiæ Dei a fratribus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παῦλος δὲ ἐπιλεξάμενος σιλᾶν ἐξῆλθεν παραδοθεὶς τῇ χάριτι τοῦ θεοῦ ὑπὸ τῶν ἀδελφῶν
verses.king_james: And Paul chose Silas, and departed, being recommended by the brethren unto the grace of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Perambulabat autem Syriam et Ciliciam, confirmans ecclesias: præcipiens custodire præcepta Apostolorum et seniorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διήρχετο δὲ τὴν συρίαν καὶ κιλικίαν ἐπιστηρίζων τὰς ἐκκλησίας
verses.king_james: And he went through Syria and Cilicia, confirming the churches.
